title: Looking Back at the NLP Journey
concept: The progression from simple word counters to contextual language encoders changed the entire field of natural language processing. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: NLP Ethics and Bias.'

exercises.prompt: Which field of AI focuses on helping computers understand human text, field of AI is dedicated to helping computers understand human text and technology focuses on helping computers understand human written and spoken human language?
exercises.explain: Natural Language Processing (NLP) is the branch of AI that helps computers read, understand, and interpret human languages.

cards.title: Bridging the Gap
cards.body: Think about how you talk to friends compared to how early computers read text. Early systems just counted words like beans in a jar. Today, computers can grasp the subtle vibes, humor, and hidden meanings in our sentences. Let's see how we got here!

cards.title: What is a Contextual Encoder?
cards.body: Contextual language encoders are advanced AI models that read entire sentences at once. Instead of treating words as isolated units, they analyze how words relate to their neighbors to capture true meaning and nuance.

cards.title: The Reading Companion
cards.body: Imagine you are reading a book with a basic dictionary. A simple word counter only looks up each word individually without caring about the story. A contextual encoder is like an experienced reader who understands that the word 'bank' means something completely different in 'river bank' versus 'money bank'.

cards.title: Context in Action
cards.body: In the past, a search engine might flag the sentence 'I want to run a program' under 'sports' because of the word 'run'. Today's contextual encoders easily recognize that 'run' and 'program' together refer to computer software.

cards.title: Do They Really Think?
cards.body: Many people think that because AI models can read context, they actually think like human beings. In reality, they are just incredibly good at calculating how words relate to each other based on massive amounts of reading practice, not actual consciousness.

cards.title: Then vs. Now
cards.body: See how the approach to reading the word 'crane' has evolved from simple counting to context mapping.

left.text: Sees 'crane' as just one count. Cannot tell if it is a bird or a construction machine.

right.text: Looks at surrounding words like 'nest' or 'steel' to decide the exact meaning of 'crane'.

exercises.prompt: How did early natural language processing systems analyze text?

options.text: They analyzed the emotional tone of every sentence.
They counted word frequencies without looking at context.
They translated sentences into multiple languages instantly.
They understood sarcasm and humor perfectly.

exercises.explain: Early systems simply counted how many times a word appeared (frequency) rather than looking at the surrounding words (context).

exercises.prompt: Early NLP word counters kept track of the exact order and relationship of all words in a sentence.
exercises.explain: Older systems like 'Bag-of-Words' ignored word order completely, which is why context was easily lost.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how AI understands word meaning.
exercises.sentence: Modern AI looks at the surrounding words to understand the true _____ of a word.
exercises.explain: Context is what allows modern NLP models to tell the difference between 'river bank' and 'savings bank'.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence demonstrates a modern AI model understanding the context of the word 'Apple'?

options.text: I went to Apple to buy some fresh red apples for baking a pie.
I went to Apple to purchase the latest smartphone and upgrade my cloud storage.
I went to Apple to pick fruit off the branches in the orchard.

exercises.explain: Option B is generated by a modern contextual model because it correctly understands that 'apple' refers to the technology company in this context, whereas Option A and Option C make literal fruit assumptions.

exercises.prompt: What was the major shift in how NLP systems evolved over time to understand language?

options.text: They stopped using neural networks entirely.
They shifted focus from counting isolated words to understanding word meaning in context.
They began ignoring the order of words in sentences.
They decided to only analyze numerical data instead of text.

exercises.explain: By mastering how NLP evolved from basic counting to deep contextual understanding, you are now ready to explore how these systems affect society. Next up: NLP Ethics and Bias.
